sections.paragraph:
Ἰουδαία, ας, ἡ (יְהוּדָה; but the word is to be derived fr. Aram. יְהוּדַי; fr. the adj. Ἰουδαῖος with γῆ or χώρα supplied, as Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 281) ‘Judea’ (since Clearchus, the pupil of Aristotle: Fgm. 6 [ in Jos., C. Ap. 1, 179]; ins [Schürer II 1 n. 2]; PRyl 189, 5; LXX; Philo).

① the southern part of Palestine in contrast to Samaria, Galilee, Perea and Idumea, Judea (s. Mk 3:7f; Ac 9:31; so LXX and oft. Joseph., Just. Also Strabo 16, 2, 34 w. Galil. and Samar.) Mt 2:1, 5, 22; 3:1; 4:25; 24:16; Mk 3:7; 13:14; Lk 1:65; 2:4; 3:1; 5:17; 6:17; 21:21; J 4:3, 47, 54; 7:1, 3; 11:7; Ac 1:8; 8:1; 9:31; 12:19; 15:1; 21:10; 28:21; Ro 15:31; 2 Cor 1:16; Gal 1:22; AcPl Ha 8, 29f=BMM verso 2. Metaph. of the inhabitants Mt 3:5 (Ἰ. χώρα Mk 1:5; see Ἰουδαῖος 1).—Buhl 64–75; HGuthe, RE IX 556–85; XXIII 713f (lit.); BHHW II 901; YAharoni, The Land of the Bible2 ’79; MStern, in CRINT I/1, 308–76.

② Judea, broadly understood as the region occupied by the people of Israel, Judea =‘land of the Judeans (Jews)’, i.e. Palestine (Nicol. Dam. [I b.c.]: 90 Fgm. 96 Jac. [in Jos., Ant. 14, 9]; Diod S 40, 3, 2; Strabo 16, 2, 34; Memnon [I b.c. / I a.d.]: 434 Fgm. 1, 18, 9 Jac.; Ptolem. 5, 16, 1; cp. 15, 6–8 and Apotelesmatica 2, 3, 29 and 31. Cass. Dio 37, 16; 47, 28; Tacitus, Hist. 5, 9, 1; LXX; EpArist 4 and12; Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 200; Joseph.; Just., D. 32, 4.—On the NT: ELevesque, Vivre et Penser 3, ’43/44, 104–11 denies the wider use) Lk 1:5; 4:44 (v.l. Γαλιλαίας, s. the entry, end); 7:17; 23:5; Ac 10:37; 11:1, 29; 1 Th 2:14. πᾶσα ἡ χώρα τῆς Ἰ. the whole Judean (Jewish) country Ac 26:20. εἰς τὰ ὅρια τῆς Ἰ. πέραν τοῦ Ἰορδάνου into the Judean (Jewish) territory beyond the Jordan Mt 19:1; cp. Mk 10:1. On the mention of Judea Ac 2:9 cp. the variants and conjectures in Nestle; EvDobschütz, ZWT 45, 1902, 407–10; Harnack, AG 1908, 65f; SKrauss, ZDPV 33, 1910, 225; OLagercrantz, Eranos 10, 1910, 58–60; LKöhler, ET 22, 1911, 230f. Also BZ 1, 1903, 219; 7, 1909, 219; 9, 1911, 218; ZNW 9, 1908, 253f; 255f; Haenchen ad loc.; TRE XXV 591–96.—OEANE III 253–57. M-M. EDNT. TW.

  1. fr. = from

adj. adj. = adjective

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

oft. oft. = often

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Buhl Buhl = FB., Geographie v. Palästina—List 6

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

CRINT CRINT = Compendia Rerum Iudaicarum ad Novum Testamentum—List 6

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Memnon Memnon (of Heracleon?), I b.c.–I? a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

NT NT = New Testament

Vivre et Penser Vivre et Penser —List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

ZWT ZWT = Zeitschrift für Wissenschaftliche Theologie—List 6

AG AG = Apostelgeschichte (in German titles relating to Acts)

ZDPV ZDPV = Zeitschrift der Deutschen Palästina-Vereins—List 6

Eranos Eranos , Acta Philologica Suecana—List 6

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

BZ BZ = Biblische Zeitschrift—List 6

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

TRE TRE = Theologische Realenzyklopädie—List 6

OEANE OEANE = Oxford Encyclopedia of Archaeology in the Near East—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 477
